findings.message: New public endpoint count_items() has no docstring explaining its purpose, response shape, or the relationship to /items. Public route handlers in this file (e.g. get_items) document the contract; this one should too.
findings.suggestion: Add a docstring describing the endpoint, e.g. "Return the total number of items as {\"count\": <int>} — convenience counterpart to GET /items."

findings.message: The response is typed as a bare `dict` with no response_model, so the OpenAPI contract for /items/count is unspecified (any shape). Sibling endpoint /items uses a typed response_model (ItemRead); this asymmetry will produce a vague OpenAPI spec and may cause the contract diff CI job to under-document the new route.
findings.suggestion: Define a small response model (e.g. `class ItemCount(BaseModel): count: int`) and either annotate the return type as `ItemCount` or set `response_model=ItemCount` on the route decorator.

findings.message: README.md's Endpoints section lists /items but not the new /items/count endpoint, so the user-facing endpoint documentation is now stale.
findings.suggestion: Add a bullet under README.md "## Endpoints": `- `GET /items/count` — item count for the seeded/listed items`.

findings.message: Implementation loads all items into memory just to take len(), which is non-obvious and undocumented. If the intent is a cheap count, a SQL COUNT would be expected; if the intent is deliberately reusing list_items, a one-line comment would make that explicit so future readers don't "optimize" it.
findings.suggestion: Either add a brief comment (e.g. `# Reuses list_items for parity with GET /items; swap to a COUNT(*) query if the table grows.`) or replace with a repository-level count helper and document it.
